user_message: Working on Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery's escalation triage. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me map the stakeholders.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 10:33 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 11:32 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 12:33 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-G trap (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-G failure mode (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-G -- Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence
